title: Encapsulate DepKey to enforce no self-loops by construction

description:
**Problem**
Running `cargo run --example session_coordinator_boundary -- check` reports a counterexample for the property "pending ingest starts at next expected". The trace shows pending ingest can start ahead of the next expected seq:
- DeliverEvent(3)
- DeliverEvent(2)
- DeliverEvent(1)
- CoordinatorIngest
- DeliverEvent(1)
- DeliverEvent(2)
- DeliverAck

This indicates either the model invariant is wrong or the coordinator boundary logic allows a gap to slip through.

**Design**
Inspect `beads_stateright_models/examples/session_coordinator_boundary.rs` and the underlying boundary logic. Confirm the intended rule (pending ingest should start at next expected, not beyond). If the rule is intended, adjust the model and/or coordinator state machine so it enforces contiguity when ingesting pending events. Add a regression test in the beads runtime to mirror the counterexample trace once the implementation exists (or add a model-only regression property if implementation work is not yet wired).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Model check passes without the "pending ingest starts at next expected" counterexample.
- [ ] If rule is intended, coordinator boundary enforces contiguity (no ingest beyond next expected).
- [ ] Regression test added for the counterexample trace (model or implementation).

**Files:** beads_stateright_models/examples/session_coordinator_boundary.rs
title: Stateright model: pending ingest contiguity counterexample

description: Add/expand property tests asserting commutativity, associativity, and idempotence for key Crdt implementors to improve confidence in merge determinism.
title: Add property tests for Crdt::join law coverage across implementations

description:
**Problem**
`cargo xtest` currently fails in `beads-rs::e2e e2e_replicated_fsync_timeout_receipt` with `durability wait exceeded 2000 steps`. The failure reproduces with `cargo test -p beads-rs --features e2e-tests --test e2e -- e2e_replicated_fsync_timeout_receipt --exact --nocapture`. In `crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/e2e.rs`, `ReplicationRig::await_durability` compares `Instant::now()` against `wait_timeout` while the harness itself advances a simulated clock via `advance_ms(1)`. On a fast machine the loop can burn through all 2000 simulated steps before 50ms of wall clock elapse, so the timeout receipt path is never reached.

**Design**
Make deterministic durability waits in the e2e rig use the rig clock rather than wall clock. The timeout proof should be driven by the same simulated time that `pump()` and `advance_ms()` control, so the test stays stable regardless of host speed. Add or adjust a regression test so `e2e_replicated_fsync_timeout_receipt` reliably returns `ProtocolErrorCode::DurabilityTimeout` with a pending receipt instead of panicking on the step cap.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `cargo test -p beads-rs --features e2e-tests --test e2e -- e2e_replicated_fsync_timeout_receipt --exact --nocapture` returns the expected durability-timeout response instead of panicking
- [ ] `ReplicationRig::await_durability` no longer depends on wall-clock `Instant` for deterministic timeout behavior
- [ ] `cargo xtest` is green again on the closing tree

**Files**
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/testkit/e2e.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/e2e.rs`
title: Deterministic e2e durability timeout uses wall clock instead of rig time
